response.justification: The candidate is a versatile web and full-stack developer with tangential geospatial exposure through the GISCollective and Green Map GIS projects, but critically lacks the C++ or Rust proficiency that is the primary technical requirement for this routing tile pipeline role. Their C/C++ experience is limited to a single past role writing LabWindows components and a bachelor thesis audio project — neither recent nor relevant to geospatial routing. Their geospatial work covers NGO-focused data collection, not routing optimization, tile generation, or navigation pipelines. Distributed systems exposure exists at the HTTP/microservices layer rather than the fault-tolerant, consistency-focused infrastructure this role demands.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity through GISCollective platform and Green Map GIS application, including a FOSS4G conference talk
Demonstrated low-latency systems work using DLang with async I/O, caching, fibers, and high-performance databases
Breadth of systems exposure (embedded, robotic arms, mobile, distributed microservices) indicating adaptability across technical domains
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience; primary production stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang with only dated and peripheral C/C++ exposure
No routing tile generation, navigation pipeline, or map data transformation experience at any scale
Geospatial work is scoped to NGO data collection tools, not production routing systems with real-time traffic integration or global reliability requirements

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation pipeline experience
